epigraph.text:
Truly I tell you, if you have faith as small as a 
mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, `Move from here to there,' 
and it will move.
epigraph.quote: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.

content.text: {The only way to do great work is to love what you do.}{Steve Jobs}

Multiplying polynomials extends the distributive property. The key idea 
is simple: multiply each term in one polynomial by each term in the 
other, then combine like terms.

When multiplying monomials, multiply the coefficients and use the 
product rule for exponents.
Multiplying a Monomial and a Polynomial

Use the distributive property: multiply the monomial by each term of 
the polynomial.

When multiplying two binomials, use the distributive property twice. 
The FOIL method provides a helpful mnemonic.

content.title: FOIL Method
content.content:
For $(a + b)(c + d)$:

- **F**irst: $a \cdot c$

- **O**uter: $a \cdot d$

- **I**nner: $b \cdot c$

- **L**ast: $b \cdot d$
Then add all four products.

content.text:
For polynomials with more than two terms, use the extended distributive 
property: multiply each term in the first polynomial by each term in 
the second.

content.title: Trinomial times trinomial
content.solution:
Multiply each term in the first by each term in the second:
$$
 &= x^2(x^2 - x + 3) + 2x(x^2 - x + 3) + (-1)(x^2 - x + 3) \\
 &= x^4 - x^3 + 3x^2 + 2x^3 - 2x^2 + 6x - x^2 + x - 3 \\
 &= x^4 + x^3 + 0x^2 + 7x - 3 \\
 &= x^4 + x^3 + 7x - 3
$$

marginNotes.text: Be fruitful and increase in number.
